title: The Loop Semgrep Rule Schema (Phase A)
description: Defines the structure of a single Semgrep rule in theloop-rules.yml

id.description: Rule identifier (kebab-case). Must be globally unique. Example: injection-001-sql-string-concat

languages.description: Target languages for this rule. Use 'generic' for language-agnostic patterns (e.g., regex-based secret detection).

message.description: Human-readable message shown to developer in PR comment. Should include incident ID, severity, and brief remediation hint.

severity.description: ERROR = blocks merge. WARNING = informational only.

incident_id.description: Reference to incident in The Loop database. Example: injection-001

category.description: Incident category from The Loop taxonomy.

loop_url.description: URL to incident details. Phase A: placeholder URL (https://loop.oute.pro/incidents/{incident_id}). Phase B: actual incident detail page.

remediation.description: Step-by-step remediation instructions for developer. Must be actionable and specific to the vulnerability.

patterns.description: Semgrep pattern definitions. Can include pattern, pattern-either, pattern-regex, pattern-not, etc. See Semgrep documentation for details.

pattern-not.description: Optional: patterns to exclude from match. Reduces false positives.

exclude.description: File path patterns to exclude from scanning. Example: ['*.test.*', 'test_*', 'tests/', '.env.example']

examples.message: [The Loop] SQL injection via string concatenation detected. Incident: injection-001 | Severity: CRITICAL

metadata.remediation: Use parameterized queries: cursor.execute(query, (value,)) in Python; prepared statements in JavaScript.
